title: Strings 4
description: Understanding the difference between `String` and `&str` is essential. This exercise tests that understanding.
instructions: Fix each function call to pass the correct string type.
hints: You can convert a String to &str by taking a reference with `&`.
Change to: string_slice(&"abc".to_uppercase()); or use the `string` function instead.
explanation: Understanding when you have a `String` vs `&str` is crucial in Rust. String literals are `&str`. Methods like `.to_string()`, `String::from()`, `.to_owned()`, and `format!()` all produce `String`. Methods like `.to_uppercase()` also return `String`. You can pass a `&String` where `&str` is expected thanks to Deref coercion.
